evals.prompt: I just finished adding a new `formatCurrency(amount, locale)` function to my npm package `tiny-fmt`. It's in src/index.ts and I've already written the implementation. Now I need to go through the full release process — lint, test, build, changelog, version bump, diff review, and push. Walk me through it and do what you can. The package is on the main branch.
evals.expected_output: Agent walks through all 8 steps in order: lint, test (noting new tests needed for formatCurrency), build, CHANGELOG update (moving Unreleased to versioned entry), npm version minor, diff review for DRY/dead code/README drift, final npm test && npm run build, git push && git push --tags, and npm publish (since we're on main). Agent flags that README should be updated to document the new formatCurrency API.
expectations: Agent runs or instructs to run lint as the first step
Agent explicitly notes that tests must be written for the new formatCurrency function before proceeding
Agent updates or instructs updating CHANGELOG.md, moving Unreleased content to a versioned entry
Agent runs or instructs npm version with an appropriate bump (minor for new feature)
Agent performs a diff review step checking for DRY violations, dead code, and README drift
Agent runs or instructs a final npm test && npm run build before pushing
Agent runs or instructs git push && git push --tags
Agent runs or instructs npm publish (since on main branch)
Agent notes that README.md should be updated to document the new formatCurrency API

evals.prompt: I just created a new npm package called `qr-lite` that generates QR codes. I have the source code in src/ and a package.json with name and version, but nothing else yet. Before I do anything, what files do I need to create and what should they contain? Help me set up the full baseline.
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies all missing baseline files: README.md (with correct structure: one-liner, Install, Quick Start, API, Contributing, License), CHANGELOG.md (with Unreleased section at top), LICENSE file (MIT by default), and checks/fixes package.json for missing required fields (description, main/exports, scripts, repository, license, keywords, files array). Agent creates or provides content for each.
expectations: Agent identifies README.md as missing and creates or provides a correctly structured one (one-liner value prop, Install, Quick Start, API, Contributing, License sections)
Agent identifies CHANGELOG.md as missing and creates one with an [Unreleased] section at the top in Keep-a-Changelog format
Agent identifies LICENSE as missing and creates or instructs creating one (MIT or appropriate)
Agent checks package.json and notes any missing required fields (description, main/exports, scripts, repository, license, keywords, files)
Agent does NOT create unnecessary extra files beyond the baseline requirements

evals.prompt: I'm on the feature branch `feat/add-retry-logic` for my npm package `axios-resilient`. I just finished implementing the retry logic and want to publish the new version to npm right now so my colleague can test it. Can you help me publish?
evals.expected_output: Agent firmly but clearly explains that publishing from a feature branch is not allowed by the workflow. It should explain why (branch publishes bypass review gates and create untraceable releases), then guide the user to: run the quality checks on the branch (lint, test, build), open a PR to merge into main, and only run npm publish from main after merging.
expectations: Agent detects or notes that the user is on a feature branch (feat/add-retry-logic) and not on main/master
Agent explicitly refuses or warns against running npm publish from the feature branch
Agent explains why publishing from a feature branch is problematic
Agent provides a path forward: run quality checks on the branch, open a PR, merge to main, then publish from main
Agent does NOT run or instruct npm publish while on the feature branch
